target.title: fix(thinking): let authoritative provider profiles override stale catalog reasoning=false
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #84880. Subagent spawns and `/think` validation reject non-`off` thinking on canonical `openai/*` and `openai-codex/*` GPT-5 reasoning models when a stale catalog entry has `reasoning: false`. `resolveThinkingProfile` short-circuits to off-only on `reasoning === false` before consulting the provider plugin profile, so persisted catalogs that lag behind runtime augmentation force `Use one of: off.` even for models the provider knows support `high`/`xhigh`.

Introduces an `overridesCatalogReasoning?: boolean` flag on `ProviderThinkingProfile`. When a provider returns a profile with this flag set, the resolver treats the plugin as the canonical source and uses its level list ahead of the catalog opt-out. Providers opt in **per model**, so non-reasoning models (e.g. `openai/chat-latest`, `openai/gpt-5.3-chat-latest`) still honor catalog `reasoning: false`.

The OpenAI extension opts in for GPT-5 reasoning model refs and explicitly excludes non-reasoning chat-latest variants. The classifier is **suffix-aware**: any `*-chat-latest` model is catalog-authoritative unless it appears in the explicit `OPENAI_REASONING_CHAT_LATEST_MODEL_IDS = ["gpt-5.1-chat-latest", "gpt-5.2-chat-latest"]` opt-in list (matches bundled catalog reasoning flags in `extensions/openai/openclaw.plugin.json`).

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: `sessions_spawn(model="openai-codex/gpt-5.5", thinking="high"|"xhigh")` and `sessions_spawn(model="openai/gpt-5.4", thinking="high")` are rejected with `Thinking level "high" is not supported for openai/gpt-5.5. Use one of: off.` even though the canonical OpenAI/Codex catalog lists these models as reasoning-capable — see live v2026.5.20 repro logs in [issue #84880](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/84880). The patch reorders `resolveThinkingProfile` so an authoritative provider profile (now flagged via `overridesCatalogReasoning`) wins over stale persisted catalog `reasoning: false` rows.

**Real environment tested**: macOS 25.5.0 (Darwin arm64), Node 24.13.0, pnpm 11.1.0; repository checked out at `fix/subagent-thinking-overrides-catalog-reasoning` head `69986fc5` rebased on `origin/main` `f483f59b`; runtime probe driving the patched modules end-to-end through the same `resolveOpenAIThinkingProfile` / `resolveOpenAICodexThinkingProfile` entry points that the live Gateway calls on every `sessions_spawn` validation in `src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-run.ts:794` and `src/agents/subagent-spawn.ts:1172`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
```
git checkout fix/subagent-thinking-overrides-catalog-reasoning
node --import tsx probe-thinking.mjs
```

The probe is a small driver script that imports the patched `extensions/openai/thinking-policy.ts` and calls the classifier for each acceptance scenario from #84880 plus the chat-latest regression guards from the ClawSweeper review, then checks the resolved `levels[]` and `overridesCatalogReasoning` flag against expected values.

**Evidence after fix**: Live terminal output from `node --import tsx probe-thinking.mjs` after applying the patch — copied verbatim, no redaction needed since the probe data is synthetic:

```
=== Runtime probe: extensions/openai/thinking-policy.ts ===

PASS | [#84880 §1] openai/gpt-5.4 high
       levels=[off, minimal, low, medium, high, xhigh] overridesCatalogReasoning=true (want true)
PASS | [#84880 §2] openai-codex/gpt-5.5 high
       levels=[off, minimal, low, medium, high, xhigh] overridesCatalogReasoning=true (want true)
PASS | [#84880 §3] openai-codex/gpt-5.5 xhigh
       levels=[off, minimal, low, medium, high, xhigh] overridesCatalogReasoning=true (want true)
PASS | openai/gpt-5.5 minimal/low/medium/high/xhigh
       levels=[off, minimal, low, medium, high, xhigh] overridesCatalogReasoning=true (want true)
PASS | [ClawSweeper P1] openai/chat-latest
       levels=[off, minimal, low, medium, high] overridesCatalogReasoning=false (want false)
PASS | [ClawSweeper P1] openai/gpt-5-chat-latest
       levels=[off, minimal, low, medium, high] overridesCatalogReasoning=false (want false)
PASS | [ClawSweeper P1] openai/gpt-5.3-chat-latest
       levels=[off, minimal, low, medium, high] overridesCatalogReasoning=false (want false)
PASS | openai/gpt-5.1-chat-latest (reasoning catalog row)
       levels=[off, minimal, low, medium, high] overridesCatalogReasoning=true (want true)
PASS | openai/gpt-5.2-chat-latest (reasoning catalog row)
       levels=[off, minimal, low, medium, high, xhigh] overridesCatalogReasoning=true (want true)
PASS | openai/o3-mini
       levels=[off, minimal, low, medium, high] overridesCatalogReasoning=true (want true)
PASS | openai/gpt-5-pro
       levels=[off, minimal, low, medium, high] overridesCatalogReasoning=true (want true)
PASS | openai/gpt-4.1
       levels=[off, minimal, low, medium, high] overridesCatalogReasoning=false (want false)

Summary: all 12 probes PASS.
```

**Observed result after fix**: 12/12 probes pass. For the canonical GPT-5 reasoning model refs the patch flips `overridesCatalogReasoning` to `true` and surfaces the full `[off, minimal, low, medium, high]` level set (plus `xhigh` on the appropriate models), so a stale catalog row marked `reasoning: false` no longer triggers the off-only short-circuit. For non-reasoning chat-latest variants (`chat-latest`, `gpt-5-chat-latest`, `gpt-5.3-chat-latest`) and legacy non-reasoning models (`gpt-4.1`) the classifier returns `overridesCatalogReasoning=false`, so a stale catalog `reasoning: false` row still wins and the runtime keeps the off-only path documented by the catalog opt-out.

Cross-check against the bundled `extensions/openai/openclaw.plugin.json` shows the classifier verdict matches the catalog truth for every chat-latest variant in the bundle:

| Model | Catalog reasoning | Classifier verdict | Stale-row override |
|---|---|---|---|
| `gpt-5-chat-latest` | `false` | non-authoritative | catalog wins (off-only) |
| `gpt-5.1-chat-latest` | `true` | authoritative | overrides stale `false` |
| `gpt-5.2-chat-latest` | `true` | authoritative | overrides stale `false` |
| `gpt-5.3-chat-latest` | `false` | non-authoritative | catalog wins (off-only) |
| `chat-latest` | `false` | non-authoritative | catalog wins (off-only) |
| `gpt-5.5`, `gpt-5.4`, `gpt-5-codex`, `o3-mini`, … | `true` | authoritative | overrides stale `false` |
| `gpt-4.1`, `gpt-4o`, … | `false` | non-authoritative | catalog wins (off-only) |

**What was not tested**: A redacted `sessions_spawn` live run against a custom-built Gateway binary in my deployment — the probe exercises the same classifier and resolver entry points the live Gateway calls, but I did not build and install a custom Gateway binary into my OrbStack VM for this PR. Also out of scope: the Ollama Cloud variant raised by @SplyzerRB in #84880 where the subagent ignores explicit `thinking: "off"` — that path forwards `thinking` even when the spawn arg is `off` and is a separate runtime bug that does not share a code path with the OpenAI/Codex catalog-override issue this PR fixes.

## What changes

- **`src/plugins/provider-thinking.types.ts`** — add `overridesCatalogReasoning?: boolean` to `ProviderThinkingProfile`.
- **`src/auto-reply/thinking.ts`** — reorder `resolveThinkingProfile`: when the plugin profile is authoritative, it wins over catalog `reasoning: false`; otherwise the existing off-only short-circuit applies.
- **`extensions/openai/thinking-policy.ts`** — suffix-aware classifier for chat-latest variants; flags reasoning model refs as authoritative via `isOpenAIReasoningModelId`.
- **`docs/tools/thinking.md`** and **`docs/plugins/sdk-migration.md`** — document the new opt-in flag.
- **`docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256`** — regenerated via the API baseline generator.

Additionally — **`fix(plugins): break plugin-registry-snapshot ↔ plugin-metadata-snapshot.types import cycle`** (`69986fc5`):
- Pre-existing madge cycle on main introduced by upstream `bf64de91`. Verified by reverting this PR's diff and re-running the architecture check on plain `origin/main` — cycle still reproduces.
- Extract `PluginRegistrySnapshotSource` to a new leaf module `src/plugins/plugin-registry-snapshot.types.ts`; `plugin-registry-snapshot.ts` re-exports the same name so no downstream import changes. Happy to split into a separate PR if maintainers prefer.

## Notes

- Related to PR #84902 (NianJiuZst), which takes the same architectural direction. This PR is an independent attempt with additional regression-guard tests covering the `gpt-5.3-chat-latest` chat-latest variant raised by ClawSweeper review. Happy to defer to or close in favor of #84902 if maintainers prefer that thread.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
target.author: aaajiao
